Black Orchid Tom Ford for women

Tom Ford

Search by accords
User Ratings
Rating
When To Wear

Perfume rating 3.89 out of 5 with 31,179 votes

Reviews (3.1K)

Black Orchid by Tom Ford is a Oriental Floral fragrance for women. Black Orchid was launched in 2006. Black Orchid was created by David Apel and Pierre Negrin. Top notes are Truffle, Gardenia, Black Currant, Ylang-Ylang, Jasmine, Bergamot, Mandarin Orange and Amalfi Lemon; middle notes are Orchid, Spices, Gardenia, Fruity Notes, Ylang-Ylang, Jasmine and Lotus; base notes are Mexican chocolate, Patchouli, Vanille, Incense, Amber, Sandalwood, Vetiver and White Musk.

Black Orchid was created by perfumers of the fragrance company Givaudan in 2006. It was announced as an oriental chypre. The top notes are French jasmine, black truffle, ylang-ylang, black currant, and effervescent citrus. In the floral-spicy heart, dwells Tom Ford's black orchid, more imaginary than real, and the base combines woodsy notes (patchouli and sandalwood), dark chocolate, incense, amber, vetiver, vanilla, and balsam.

Black Orchid is opulent and classically dark; its fragrance is in line with the best creations of the best perfume houses. A luxurious and sensual fragrance with a rich, dark trace of black orchid and spices, it is modern and timeless. It remains to be said that Black Orchid is long lasting and with a widespread aura.

"This is not about an ordinary orchid, it's about something a little more strange and rare. I wanted the blackest orchid, and those aren't easy to find." The perfume is luxurious and the product range includes Eau de Parfum in 50 ml (at US$90) and 100 ml (at US$135) bottles, and a limited edition of 5,000 15 ml bottles of Parfum at US$600. The distribution is limited.

Read about this perfume in other languages: Deutsch, Español, Français, Italiano, Magyar, Nederlands, Polski, Português, Română, Srpski, Svenska, Türkçe, Čeština, Ελληνικά, Монгол, Русский, Українська, עברית, العربية, 日本語, 汉语, 한국어.

All Reviews By Date

Igalmusic85

This is bold, confident, sexy, mysterious scent. Black Orchid truly is one of a kind masterpiece. I absolutely love it. I would wear it anytime, anywhere, even during summer, I don't care. I don't ever want to be without this magical juice.

kage121 03/17/26 11:04

so pungent even my dog puked, WAY too strong for human consumption.

duisenbayev.dn 03/17/26 00:44

Dark Fantasy in the bottle.

Fcking Awesome

wndes 03/15/26 14:19

Off the opening you would think this is much more harsh then it really is, but once you get to the drydown this is soooooooooooo smooth and enjoyable.

Moaaz_mms 03/10/26 10:28

An amazing fragrance, picture a dark, goth, type of smell. I pick up mainly the orchid, dark chocoloate, and patchouli. This is NOT for everyone. It can smell very earthy and that is something i like about it because it adds character. Picture a very nice muddy chocolate lava cake with orchid flowers ontop of it. Definetely a favourite.

velvetulipe 03/06/26 08:19

Wow... this perfume is so beautiful. It's truly special, and I agree with people saying that if you usually wear light, more standard perfumes, perhaps avoid trying this one too soon.

The initial notes are truly explosive: mushroom, earth, and spice. It's strong and quite impressive the first time you smell it. But it develops beautifully. A slightly soapy scent emerges, undoubtedly the gardenia and ylang-ylang. Then come fresh, tangy, slightly green, and, yes, chocolatey notes. But a very dark and full-bodied chocolate (probably the patchouli) with a touch of smoky incense. Everything softens, with amber, ripe fruit, and those initial notes that create a distant yet ever-present memory.

It's not the kind of perfume I usually wear, but since I first experienced it, I've wanted nothing more than to add it to my collection. It transports me in a way that very few perfumes have before.

In a comments someone imagined Morticia Addams wearing it, and that's exactly right.

Here's the image that comes to my mind... We enter a Victorian house, decorated entirely in black and purple. Morticia leads us to her orchid greenhouse, and all the scents hit us as soon as we enter. The slightly prickly damp earth, the smell of mushrooms, the green stems she's just cut from her precious black orchids. That sweet, amber floral scent.

It's just so beautiful... Truly a masterpiece.

So, at Tom Ford, they say the notes are as follows (which seem a little different here):
Black Truffle
Ylang-ylang
Black Orchid
Black Plum
Rum Absolute
Patchouli

PeakWaterlillyvegeta 03/05/26 02:51

I'm so glad I didn't spray it on my skin. It made me gag with how strong and heavy it was, plus, I absolutely hate truffles.
Massive props to whoever can pull this off, and I do admit that I can see why some people love it, and how they would wear it.
Get it if you're really into strong dark smells, not a safe blind buy at all, and I can't say much about duration since I sprayed it on paper, however, it was projecting like crazy.
Overall 2.5/10 for me personally, but a 9/10 for those of you who can wear it with confidence.

cemeteryroses 03/01/26 09:27

i have no idea whats in this god im just in love with it. i'd say this might even be my favorite fragrance, almost nothing tops the drama going on in this for me. its a mood on itself to say the least. i couldnt fucking love this thing more, just such a confident masterpiece and the best head turner there is. the sillage is just perfect too leaving a trail without being overwhelming and room filling at all.

i personally dont find this too dramatic for daily wear to be honest but that's solely because my preferences are on the bolder side. obviously goes much better with colder weathers and nighttime, maybe even in the spring as well but i'd rather skip on the summer heat, because with that sillage this could probably turn into something almost headache inducing on overly hot days.

SweetGirl85 02/28/26 02:12

Very interesting perfume. Deep, mature and seductive. I have many years to smell but i still remember its mysterious embracement. I like it a lot when i first tried it and i still like it. I think that notes like truffle they make it unique. Suitable for mature women full of confidence who know exactly what they want and how to acquire it!

ScentualEruption 02/28/26 01:20

It smells strongly of black liquorice to me. I cannot get past it. I wish I could because I like the notes listed in this fragrance.

divineperfumer 02/27/26 12:00

i absolutely hated this fragrance when i first tried it but now its one of my favorites very earthy very sexy and a little bit sweet i sprayed some of this on my hoodie to test it and two days later i smelled it on my hoodie and i was just obsessed with that earthy smoky scent trail it had i knew i had to order this one im obsessed

evekrieg 02/27/26 10:30

I don't have much to add to the conversation that hasn't already been said. I had a bottle of this that I sold in 2018 and regretted, and a colleague gave me a 30ml recently. It is masterfully blended. It isn't just a perfume that "smells like a perfume", it is distinctive and smells like itself. That is how I have been judging frags lately; does something smell distinctive enough to be recognizable. This was a bold move back when absolutely everything was fruity florals before the "niche" obsession and current gourmand fetish. It also reminds me of the days when the TF Private Blends felt really unique and quality. I need to spend more time with this one again to remember who she is as I am approaching 40.

leontocephaline 02/25/26 02:39

Needed to warm up to it, and now that I've run out my 10ml decant, I feel as that I have gained sufficient understanding of Black Orchid.

A pearlescent chalcedony in twilight. Very solemn, stately, mysterious. Undeniably a white flower fragrance, all the same, it has a resinous undercurrent that steers it away from melancholia. This is a gothic, epicene fragrance with teeth. The scent profile of someone clever and self-assured. You can get away with wearing it on a spring or fall night, provided, you have the poise to carry it.

Despite hundreds of reviews everywhere that this is nuclear, my skin drinks this particular juice up, even when I'm freshly showered and moisturized. I need several GOOD sprays of Black Orchid. Maybe you can chalk it up to olfactory fatigue, but I never catch whiffs of it on myself after the first hour, unlike other perfumes. I have received a hug while wearing it and got a passing comment that I smell nice, which does not necessarily indicate if it's solely a skin-scent for me, all the same, when I spray it on clothes, my family has commented once that it is 'strong'. Do with that what you will.

Bianca.G.Valentine 02/24/26 12:10

My evening signature scent since 2013. Stunningly sexy.

roseynymph 02/24/26 08:10

I LOVE this perfume, although i rarely wear it as i have to be in a specific mood and wear a specific outfit that would suit this perfume - this fragnance definetly has its own personality.

It is sharp and tangy due to the black current but mysterious and dark at the same time. This is something a tall dark stranger would wear ans they walk past you in a dark, wet alley with only the moon illuminating their figure..

I also love this perfume on a man! However, this can very easily be oversprayed and then it becomes loud and annoying - no longer the mysterious beauty you want to get to know more, so BE CAREFUL

blueharpy 02/21/26 07:17

The first time I tried Black Orchid, it was in a store, on a paper strip. It struck me as a dark, truffle-chocolate floral fragrance, that made me think of purple velvet. It was nice, but a bit monotone and heavy. A while later, I decided to get a decant so I could try it again, this time on my skin, and experience it more attentively... And at first spray it felt so different, I thought they had sent me the wrong fragrance.

This time, I found it far more beautiful and complex. There's a freshness in the opening, and it's more delicate and floral than how I perceived it before. The black orchid isn't a heavy, luxurious velvet, but the delicate velvet of a petal.

This perfume is marketed and interpreted as a sexy, opulent scent, but to me, it feels like the fragrance of an ethereal nocturnal nymph. Dark and alluring, even narcotic, but still weightless, drifting through the night air.

On a side note, I've concluded testing fragrances on paper strips is almost useless for really understanding their nuances. It can offer a vague, introductory idea of its character, but nothing replaces testing them on skin.

Manal87mq 02/20/26 16:39

First spray and I felt like I walked into a 2007 wedding hall😭
that scent is SUPER heavy like wearing layers on top of layers.

Bonita83 02/19/26 15:39

I reach for it often in winter, its a deep fragrance, very alluring

monkfruit1 02/19/26 07:36

What an incredible masterpiece of a fragrance, it is dark, alluring, floral, earthy and has gourmande facets that make it sooooo addicting.

Izzyest20 02/16/26 13:15

TF probably one of the best brands for those who look for Unisex blends, and Black Orchid in my eyes falls in that category despite being labled for Women. Is all I should have to say. Out of the ones in the BO family this is probably the most unisex one. As a man, I love this on a woman, as much as I love it on myself. If you are an Man, who is secure and confident, this is definitely for you. I can't say much else but that this Frag is beautifully put together. Definitely for a seductive date night, sophisticated evenings, wear this at any place you would like to stand out to others. Beautifully mysterious and dark. 10/10 recommend.

Woobsie81 02/15/26 17:42

This is a really odd perfume in that I cannot get past the truffle. It smells like truffle in a dirty old aged cheese way where it smells slightly earthy and animalic but also like food so you can kind of see the appeal but mostly it's off putting.
I feel like if you haven't cooked with truffle you wouldn't have that association and wouldn't even notice it but if you have you feel like you'd be wearing a dirty expensive cheese. Oversprayed and/or stuck in a room with this it would be downright vomit inducing.

[email protected] 02/15/26 15:16

Strong spicy fragrance. Feels old like a mansion in the countryside overlooking dark woods and a graveyard on a gloomy winter's day, but in a kinda hot way. Anise, truffle, Fall leaves, wood armoire, jasmine.

Celestya 02/15/26 09:25

So sexy on my husband. For winter and fall, I love it. Warm and cocooning. Timeless, spicy, bold!

Corvessa 02/13/26 23:22

Black Orchid hits like a nuclear bomb that devistates its surroundings with nuclear fallout with a halflife of 50 years. I had to quaranteen my sampler, because it made all of my other samples smell like Black Orchid despite not spraying it for at least 7 years in my little storage drawer.

Black Orchid is for those that want to feel like they have the power and control as they become that of a nuclear warhead. Its projection sends shockwaves of dominence to all of the mere mortals surrounding you and overpowers any projection they are bringing to the table. Inhaling this aroma is like a sick gratification which leaves you hungering for more.

I personally don't like anything that has more than a moderate sillage. I'd rather let my character be what stands out rather than the fragrance I'm wearing. This fragrance is something that could definitely work on certain subjects though... just please don't be a terrorist and use it with moderation for humanity's sake.

xxxkryst 02/13/26 00:39

Truly a masterpiece of work. I'm usually not a big fan of female fragrances but this is essentially a class and timeless one that I will never get sick of.

ana05 02/11/26 11:49

I really wanted to love it. I still like it though on paper and appreciate for its originality, considering most perfume these days smell the same.
I loved the opening! Some delightful licorice, mmm, yum and spices! I think it reminds of Jungle Elephant there. If it stayed like this, it would be mine tomorrow but...The middle, awful. Something really annoying is in there and sour. Unfortunately, it stayed till the dry down :(, less but could still smell it. The rest was nice and creamy, but that sharp note just so not agreeing with my nose and ruined everything.
I feel like all the notes seem to work pretty well apart from that one sour note...which is so disappointing. That note seems to stick out and not be cohesive with the rest. Why is it there?? Really annoys me.
I think that it's either that white musk which they put everywhere or maybe patchouli... But normally I like patchouli (in Angel it is a blast!) unless here the patchouli combination just doesn't work. Not sure what it is.

Oh, and the perfume doesn't last one me at all, I sprayed in the shop and it was gone in 2.5 -3 hours. I had to keep sniffing the wrist as I wasn't getting any waft. Perfumes evaporate quite quickly on me, but I expected more after reading reviews, this one was pretty weak on me.

What happens is, the modern metallic perfume that I can't stand, stay on me forever and can't wash it off, but the few gems that I do like, seem to disappear off my skin in no time...

BlueSpiritRecords 02/09/26 23:59

This is such a sad, miserable little fragrance. I fucking love it.

For the other goths out there: this is the one. I was skeptical originally, because people will call wearing a black t-shirt and pants goth these days. And in the fragrance world stuff gets called "gothic" for... having a rose note? But nah, this one is actually goth. It smells like if you put The Cure in a blender and turned their records into a perfume.

A flower in an empty bar, the wood floors slightly damp from the humidity of the rain pouring outside. It feels lonely. Deep blacks and purples. It cannot exist at day, because it brings the night with it.

Feminine, but not girly or womanly. Androgynous in a tender sort of way. Lithe bodies and lips. A soundtrack via smell.

It's apparently "polarizing" or whatever for normal people, but I mean it when I say every goth needs to put this to their nose at least once. The blending is an utter masterpiece. It's so ethereal and "lava-lamp-like" that it brings to mind the wall of sound layering from Loveless by MBV.

I had no idea things could smell like this. Black Orchid is truly art, and that's coming from someone who generally doesn't even like florals. It's so special that I almost don't want to spray it too often, even though it makes me feel so profoundly at peace with myself. I don't wanna go without this, and I'll be keeping it close throughout my life just like all my favorite albums.

aprilnicole 02/08/26 16:43

I like it because it's dark, off-putting and intense. Not a people pleaser. I have a big tendency to be too much of a people pleaser and a pushover, but when I wear this I feel like I tend to stand up straighter and speak more assertively. People notice.

Kursed Arcana 02/08/26 08:06

I both like it and find it disgusting. I thought it would be a lot stronger though based on how people talk about it. Kind of worried I got a fake lol

Smellyj3w 02/08/26 07:36

Why does it smell like buckwheat on paper 😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭

Lyssa.oxox 02/07/26 21:22

I love this, my only complaint is I get a huge whiff of licorice at first! Thankfully that goes away in about a minute or two then it’s gorgeous!!

zenais86 02/06/26 14:19

I received a lot of compliments! sillage is heavy and durability! opening is not that good... but wait some minute and u ll see the magic! TOP!

t2htisnina 02/05/26 09:26

Its potent earthiness feels rich and savoury even, the combination of unsweetened chocolate and truffle is truly unique. It might not be a crowd pleaser, but those who get it get it.

radi85 02/04/26 11:16

Got a 30ml bottle today. Smells very good,dark scent i get mostly patchouly and some flowers. It reminds me a bit of other patchouly based fragrances like Salvador Dali pour homme and Zino Davidoff. Performance is good. This is more masculine fragrance IMO,for a woman a mature one. If you like patchouly you should try it. A bit overpriced however.

Huckleberry 02/03/26 03:54

Early 90’s club scene after drinking too many White Russians.

httrung32 02/02/26 06:05

I’ve been catching whiffs of this scent for years—it’s so unique, so recognizable, it practically announces itself before the wearer walks into a room. I adore it, and I know exactly what fragrance it is, but I always thought of it as a lady fragrance. Last year, though, fate (and a mall sale) brought me face-to-face with Tom Ford’s Black Orchid again. I sniffed it, and boom, my olfactory senses were instantly captivated.
This fragrance on the test strip is dark, mysterious, and sensual; a creamy, flowery-sweet scent with just the right touch of earthy depth. I loved it so much, but I hesitated. I mean, I’m a guy (a gay guy, for sure, but still not usually rocking women’s fragrances). Then I thought: whatever, let's try it.
So I tried it. Four sprays. Hung out with my friends at the mall. And let me tell you—it was strong AF. Like, people three escalators away could probably smell me. Hours later, it was still going strong, hanging on like glitter after a night out. But after 1–2 hours, it settled beautifully on my skin, revealing creamy truffle and dark chocolate notes. It is so yummy and enticing to my senses.
Naturally, I bought the super cute 30 ml bottle right then and there. Throughout 2025, I wore it constantly, from traveling to hanging out, I always had good reactions and compliments from my friends and from other people. This winter, I crave it so much that I wear it to bed. I feel so cozy with this scent.
The scent is potent, so I stick to one or two sprays depending on the occasion. That little bottle is surprisingly affordable to use, considering how long it lasts. Honestly, it’s climbed into my Top 5 favorite fragrances of all time. Iconic, addictive, and polarizing, you either love it or hate it. And me? I’m obsessed.

ElysiumYouth 02/02/26 01:20

@DariaFrancesca74 You forgot to cut the "Certainly!" out of your ChatGPT answer, not just here but in some of your other "reviews" too. Seriously, the whole point of fragrances is to smell them, so what's the point of using AI to write a summary for you?

DariaFrancesca74 02/02/26 00:47

Certainly! Black Orchid by Tom Ford is not just a perfume, it is a true icon of modern perfumery. Launched almost twenty years ago, it continues to divide and fascinate with its aura of mystery and opulence. Forget the fresh citrus notes of conventional perfumes. Although lemon and bergamot are present, they are immediately overwhelmed by the truffle and blackcurrant. The opening is dense, almost "gothic", with a narcotizing gardenia which immediately announces the strong character of the fragrance. The heart is a complex mix where the black orchid (an accord created ad hoc by Tom Ford to evoke a rare and non-existent flower in nature) marries with warm spices and fruity notes. It is not an innocent sweetness; it is a mature sensuality, emphasized by lotus and ylang-ylang, which give an almost creamy and hypnotic nuance. The real masterpiece lies in the base. Mexican chocolate and patchouli create a "dark" and gourmand accord that is never cloying thanks to the incense and vetiver. This is where the perfume becomes enveloping, leaving an extraordinary sillage and persistence. Black Orchid is not for the faint of heart. It's a fragrance that "wears you" as much as you wear it. It is luxurious, eccentric and deeply theatrical. If you're looking for something discreet for the office, look elsewhere; if you are looking for an olfactory signature that leaves its mark, you have found it.

misslazarou 01/29/26 07:08

I dont know if i have a bad batch or if its a new formulation but on me, this lasts 2 or 3 hours
i absolutely love it, it works lovely on my skin

Madame Veillette 01/29/26 07:07

I've had my 100ml bottle sitting full in my cabinet for 10 years. While we hadn't matched before upon testing long ago, I recognized that something's still appealing, even if only the aesthetic and imagination of someday wearing it comfortably. Dozens of bottles have come and gone as this sat in a dark rear corner of my fragrance cabinet waiting its turn to perhaps disappear too. But no.

As my senses evolved to understand variances of scent performance in changing environments, my tastes have changed too. I can now appreciate dark, sweetly floral and spicy scents matched to seasons, weather, moods and occasions. I've also grown somewhat tired of many designers I've collected. Their scent overlap creates a bulk of redundancy which, to my nose, becomes boring and simultaneously overwhelming; a wardrobe stuffed with outfits of little variation in a shy comfort zone. Since realizing this, bottles are flying off my shelves at resale prices that make my stomach turn, but the there's little resale power for what's so common in fragrance world.

Anyway, today's the day I'm finally, fully wearing my Black Orchid and... She blooms in the imagination to a hauntingly alluring woman. The scent dresses like a look on its own; on me, it's a black silk robe with deeply earth-toned winding floral applique throughout. It's decadent and simply gorgeous. I'm so, so glad I never let go. 🖤

Ameliahssbhvbh 01/29/26 05:41

It's a surprisingly addictive scent. I can feel that subtle, sweet spiciness in there, which is nice. But it's overwhelmingly sweet, a candy type of sweet. The composition is beautiful and if you are a fruity, sweet perfume type of person, it will be perfect for you. But for most it's going to be headache inducing. Only a warm weather fragrance, I cannot imagine wearing this in autumn, maybe on a more warm winter day but definitely not something I would reach for. It is an elegant approach on a very sweet scent, which I'm sure a lot of people will appreciate, but I am not one of them. It's like a kids perfume with a hint of spiciness. It also feels like a scent your mum wore in 2011 and you never found it again. It feels like a fever dream, honestly. Not for me, but if someone wore this I would turn my head back. It's a bold choice you need to consider before wearing, it's a statement. A unique, overwhelming but respectable statement

AJ90210 01/29/26 03:41

I love smelling like chocolate I can’t lie

drugstore classics 01/28/26 08:38

For those wondering, OUD 24 HOURS by Ard Al Zaafaran is an excellent smell-alike or inspired-by scent of Black Orchid. In fact, if you can't find a tester for Black Orchid, blind buying a small amount of Oud 24 Hours is an excellent way to test the genre. But I warn you - the price of the 'real' is not necessarily worth it. I find Arabian houses do a fine job of strong and bold and loquacious, without luxury-brand buyer's regret!

As a side note, dupes and the original alike are serious about that lotus note. It's likely created by 'calone', the aroma chemical signifying aquatics and ozonics. I personally cannot wear this aroma chemical, as it goes entirely sour and harsh on my skin, distorting whatever beauty is there. Since nothing in this scent profile looks aquatic, I thought it Worth mentioning!

Thanospapa 01/25/26 10:57

Its like sexy grandma

Matmazel 01/24/26 07:29

A sticky, disgusting, synthetic scent.
The truffle scent is very dominant, overshadowing all other notes. I can't understand why it's so popular.

die2live 01/23/26 23:31

I have a full 50ml bottle and a 10ml travel bottle of this, if you are in Europe and interested in exchanging dm me :) I’m not looking for a fragrance in particular

alyannarenae 01/21/26 22:59

tom ford please stop making fragrances this was horrible

Shiron212 01/21/26 10:27

Who the hell thought that it would be a good idea to put a truffle note in a fragrance?!
It overpowers everything and makes this scent smell terrible on my skin :(

I get none of the notes from this pyramid, I just get truffle, old lady's perfume and maybe a hint of urine.
Scrubbed it off my wrist immediately after I got home and the patchouli-rum-bergamot base left behind actually smells quite pleasant and masculine, oddly enough.

Sample first, this is not a safe blind buy!!
I've read numerous times that this should smell sexy on a man, but I don't find it to be the case, if anything, it just smells weird and off-putting.

Unfortunately for me, but luckily for you, the performance is great, so if you like this scent, then don't be hesitant to buy it.

Afc_Croatian 01/21/26 08:03

Tom Ford Black Orchid in 2026.

This might be one of the most 50/50 fragrances I've come across, at least in the Designer category. You can really agree with both sides on this scent. If you read the negative reviews, they sound fair:
- "this reminds me of a wet cemetery" - honestly I can't argue with that
- "smelling this summons visuals of funerals" - I can't deny the dark vibe this scent has to it, borderline gothic

If you read the positive reviews:
- "dark and abstract, wear this with dark formal clothing" - definitely the time to wear this
- "sweetness that a handsome vampire would wear" - made me laugh but I can imagine it

There's no denying the dark vibe Black Orchid gives off. There's an earthiness that does not bother hiding, it's intoxicating, bold, with a subtle sweetness that gives an unfamiliar depth in Designer releases today.

Black Orchid is not afraid of being itself, often announcing its notes when you least expect it. One spray on the wrist and it will be picked up throughout the day. Do not even think about over-spraying this, chances are you've gone nose blind to it and now everyone around you is wondering if a vampire is near.

Wear this only if you are confident enough to do so. This fragrance will announce whatever room you enter. It must be the truffle and vanilla combo that continues projecting since that's what I pick up the most, despite the large note list. I'd like to say I enjoy this scent and can't get enough of it, but I really can. It's one of those fragrances that you won't want to wear two days in a row, you need a break in between wears.

8/10 stands the test of time.

Julianna B. S. 01/21/26 06:39

I know it might seem strange but I can never speak about this perfume on its own. I always bring Mugler's Angel into the conversation. To me they represent the same "woman", have the same conceptual archetype behind them, so to speak. I know some will get exactly what I mean. Both are genius compositions imho, but both are polarising. And I love, adore, can't get enough of these.... on others. I am not a Black Orchid/Angel woman and that's ok. These are my two beloved Ferraris, which sit in the garage and are intended for admiration only.

Rdeacon84 01/20/26 10:40

I love this despite myself. I hadn’t smelled it in years. Recently i went from a dabber sample straight to a full bottle of Andrea Maack’s Magma and fell in love, but found that when atomized, it was an instant and unbearable headache. This has the same basic makeup as Magma- citrus top, candied floral heart, patchouli base. And somehow, despite how insanely strong black orchid is, i never get a headache from it. There’s something softer and smoother here. Hits like niche but nobody has to know. Insanely overpriced though.

serandscent 01/20/26 03:05

black orchid is a fragrance with very intriguing notes on paper: dark, rich, and complex in theory.

however, on my skin and to my nose, the composition didn’t come together in a harmonious way. instead of being able to distinguish or enjoy individual notes, everything felt blurred and heavy, almost chaotic. the scent never really settled into something cohesive for me, and i couldn’t connect to it emotionally or sensorially.

surprisingly, it leaned more like a cologne than a perfume to my nose, which made the experience even more confusing given its reputation.

overall, it just didn’t work for me at all. sometimes a fragrance can sound beautiful in theory but fail to translate into something wearable, and this was one of those cases for me.

hueka 01/18/26 15:44

putrid

cannibalcures 01/16/26 08:50

oh this is DELICIOUS

lukaku2020 01/15/26 06:54

Black Orchid feels like a dark, dressed-up night-out scent. It’s rich and heavy from the start, kind of sweet but also earthy, with that chocolatey-patchouli vibe in the background. On my skin it goes from a loud, floral-spicy opening into a warm, slightly smoky drydown that sticks around forever. Not an everyday fragrance for me, but when it fits the mood, it’s unbeatable.

myriadofsins 01/15/26 06:48

The gardenia and jasmine are cloying and the base notes smell very synthetic. The dry down smells cheaper and any charm of the florals flattens out. Not a fan, washing it off asap

KaiZn 01/14/26 16:38

ain't bad but ain't good

MutMaat 01/14/26 02:12

The "Black Orchid"... Where to start? Where does it start? With an immediate enigmatic whirl of olfactory notes that amaze, confuse and enrapture. I feel Amalfi lemon, Gardenia and what is I guess supposed to be the truffle, but it does not have the stench (as you can guess I am NOT a lover of truffles in their any shape or form), somehow it has the spicy and animalic facet, that is blended so brilliantly with the rest.
There are a lot of fruits and spices, so the heart is somewhat tropical, but not as in beach cocktail or edible/gourmand fruits style, but a wonderous discovery of a new world of luscious flowers and unexplored forests with hidden treasures of pleasures unknown. I cannot discern the orchid or the lotus in the composition (I do personally believe that they never had a chance due to their gentle and etherial qualities), but ylang-ylang - oh yes!, - it is given a whole new role to play and it is re-discovered and opened up like never before.
This exotic fruity freshness, with tart spiciness and unexpected and mysterious notes which keep teasing and drawing everyone in, with questions, guesses and complements, is then followed and masterfully concluded with the smoky bitterness of chocolate, streaked with golden amber, and soft delicacy of vanilla.
I view the "Black Orchid" as not the perfume about a flower, but as a signature of Tom Ford's vision, his another black tuxedo that can be worn by both men and women, his understanding and appreciation of the human nature and its expression, his undoubted vision and unrestrained and fearless sexuality, that he taught us all to embrace and enjoy. The "Black Orchid" is an ode to freedom of being oneself and loving oneself just the way we are, it is the metaphysical bloom of each and every one's soul that is never the same or alike, and always, ALWAYS, absolutely irresistible!

Alexey Fisher 01/10/26 05:14

I love TF fragrances but that I don't understand. Not for me.

Travis_Bickle 01/10/26 00:39

This is my mother's favorite fragrance. I love the earthiness and that moldy, woody, spicy nature. One of the few women's fragrances that I'd wear.

Bambellini 01/10/26 00:18

It's packed with notes but they all come together so nicely. I thought I'd loathe this fragrance but when I actually tried it, I was convinced. You have to know how to wear it for sure but if you do, it gives an amazing aura and confidence. I got a travel size from a friend in England and every time I visit her in London, I have the literal urge to wear this. I'm afraid I've developed some kind of pavlovian response with this. It's spicy, woody, sultry and yet feminine, elegant and a kind of perfume that I imagine a well-established woman casually putting on every morning in her beautiful dressing room as her signature scent. It demands a negroni in an old English restaurant with dark wood and tweed interiors but just as much suitable for a rainy countryside walk.

Circe84 01/09/26 08:22

Peculiar mixture of evergreen forest and moldy cellar.

qawsedrf1 01/09/26 05:04

Would be perfect if it was less sweet, cloying, creamy

ognistecheetosy 01/08/26 11:51

the most glamorous and sexy buckwheat groats ever was

BlackPepper 01/06/26 10:11

Tom Ford's Black Orchid is in my opinion a spectacular perfume. Although it is marketed as a feminine fragrance, a man can (and should) wear it with full conviction. On a man, it takes on a mysterious, exquisite, and tremendously distinctive character, setting itself far apart from the conventional.

The composition is complex with a multitude of notes, but the predominant one for me, from the opening through to the dry down, is (obviously) the orchid. It's a dark, luxurious, and beautiful orchid that intertwines with the background notes—especially vanilla and spices—without them ever overpowering it. The overall effect is creamy, deep, and very seductive.

It's a potent fragrance with notable projection and exceptional longevity on skin and, especially, on clothes. It's ideal for evening occasions in autumn and winter, where its intense and sophisticated character truly shines. It's perfect for dates or strolls with an aura of mystery, but I wouldn't recommend it for formal meetings or very confined spaces, where its presence could become overwhelming.

My personal score:

Scent: 8/10
Projection: 9/10
Longevity: 10/10
Versatility: 6'5/10 (Due to its intensity and bold character)

Siempre777 01/05/26 22:25

Trying on skin, it gets nicely sweet and dark. Has that gothic feel. Cant wait to try the other flankers. I would’ve hated this early on in my fragrance hobby.

eau de solace 01/03/26 11:14

Black Orchid smells like how someone would imagine a dark flower petal would smell like, and i'm not getting any of the stems or leaves from this, which i always prefer not getting from floral fragrances. There is a slight chocolate nuance to it that i like from the opening but it fades pretty quickly. I'm getting images of a color in my head when i sniff this, that is very dark maybe a black purple. I can definitely see why many men are opting for the parfum version, bc the edp seems simplistic and also a bit too feminine-leaning. From what I have heard the parfum is more masculine and robust than the edp but i have never tested it personally. This seems gothic in nature, it might be something I would want to wear when i want to feel mysterious and dark, or if i want to give off that vibe when i'm in that mood. This would be a really good halloween scent imo. I would rate Black Orchid an 8.5/10, it's really good, but this might not be suitable for all people.

Dianaz 01/01/26 22:19

This really wasn’t what I expected when I smelt it, its too woody, spicy and for some reason earthy like dirt, you can’t blame me I’ve already forgotten half of the notes cause there’s too much going on, it’s the type of scent you get sick of easily, it wasn’t special especially for the price point, but overall this is definitely for someone who loves gothic smells at winter times.
2/10- Only because it’s powerful and long lasting!

vsecretangelbea 12/31/25 09:57

probably not everyone's cup of tea. smells like emily from corpse bride, like very strong floral incense mixed with dirt.

CelesteMorie 12/28/25 17:29

I have only ever laid hands first on one person, and it was a former coworker I caught attempting to steal my travel size out of my purse. I am a crazy person who wears this daily, but almost never on my skin. I usually wear it on my bra, or coat, or even my purse or dab a small bit onto my lower back, it’s insanely powerful but absolutely perfect if you can use it well. I want to drown myself in this scent. It is an ever changing, never boring complex treat! And soooo sexy. God and it lasts forever, coats I sprayed will last until they are washed, if I have a fun party weekend it never needs reapplied. It plays very well with scented lotions and conditioners, did I mention I want to drown myself in it? My one and only truest love in this world.

Karl Karlovsky 12/27/25 17:05

Smell it, boom, dark sexy confidence on woman, that got into my mind, smells more flowery to me, but dark and sexy

bulchonson 12/27/25 11:10

smells like vinegar shits RANK

marianneg23 12/26/25 14:21

I've just been gifted a bottle and I filled with dread upon taking it. In the past, my nose has read Black Orchid as 'lightly floral dirt' and left it at that. Urged by the potential guilt of not liking this gift, I set out to give it a thorough test-drive.

Black Orchid is beautiful.

And I mean that sincerely; I haven't just convinced myself I like it!

Opening, the scent is admittedly weird. The earthy truffle note does take centre stage, but as its co-stars develop, it settles into a pleasant earthiness. As the mexican chocolate, hints of spice, and vanilla come through, they meld into something very rich and flavoursome, and sweet but in no way cloying. The florals are subtle but there, far from being 'flowery'.

I could continue listing accords, they're all there, and they work in harmony, contrasting, complementing, and rounding off a very lovely, earthy fragrance. It lasts a good 10 hours on me, and dries down without losing its character. Projection is good and it gets compliments. Despite popularity, Black Orchid isn't too common and it wouldn't struggle to be unique in most crowds.

TF often fails to impress me, but, thanks to Black Orchid, I might be coming round...

naseemautefi 12/22/25 15:30

I passionately love this scent; suitable for cold days..evening scent.. outdoors.. amazing party atmosphere.. leaves a trail and is absolutely gorgeous.. so opulent and sexy beast mode.. oh what a joy! And lasts forever ❤️🔥

perfumelandsss 12/22/25 00:12

When I first smelled it in the store, I realized it wasn't the first time I'd smelled it, because I'd smelled this scent in so many places, regardless of the season... It's quite a heavy scent for me, and there's something incredibly disturbing about it, probably the fruity notes (PDM Oriana, I Hate You Baby). Beyond that, this perfume definitely isn't a scent that suits everyone or that everyone can wear (neither as a person nor in terms of the season). It's a much more mature, sophisticated, and classy scent. I think Tom Ford designed this fragrance inspired by his own personality.

PLEASE DON'T WEAR THIS PERFUME IN THE SUMMER HEAT OR SPRING; IT MAKES ME SICK AND IT DOESN'T SUIT YOU. JUST DO A LITTLE RESEARCH. (THE SAME APPLIES TO BLACK OPIUM. USING PERFUME ACCORDING TO THE SEASON IS AN ART, AND NOT EVERYONE CAN BE AN ARTIST.)

Angel Blanco 12/21/25 23:55

It took me YEARS to like this perfume, when I first sprayed it at the mall, it was no no, interesting, but way too dark, dry and masculine, how the hell is this classified as a woman's fragrance? I thought. Then I kinda forgot about it, till a vendor gave me a sampler, I tried it again at home, on dates, on nights out, and it was still a no. Interesting and classy, but too mature.
Then, this year, I finally gave it another try, I'm so in love now. Weird, isn't it?
THE SCENT: Something dark lingering between cocoa (NOT CHOCOLATE), just pure cocoa beans, unsweetened, coffee, a bit of cloves here and there, and somewhere, lurking underneath, I smell white flowers? Very luscious ones, like tuberose (tho not listed but I swear), without ever coming to the top. There's also smoke and incense very present. And then, just a tiny thiny winny bit of sweetness.
Verdict: I now find it strangely too feminine to be unisex, I smelled it on a man the other day and something seemed off, as if he'd picked wife's bottle by mistake. Although it's not for the feminine woman either, it's more of a sharp, play no games, take no prisoners type of woman. I don't see anyone in a dress or gown wearing it, think of a houndstooth tailored suit in brown and beige.
It's definitely a WINTER fragrance, since the silage is top notch.
The only person I can see wearing this everyday is the female CEO of like the biggest company in your country. To me, it's more of an occasional perfume, since although I love it, it doesn't really go with my style, physique or age. Beautiful guilty pleasure nevertheless.
Something good about it, is basic b's will not jump on it like they did with Baccarat, Santal, Delina and other I'd call "aspirational scents" to be "old money/aesthetic/different/not like other girls" because this is indeed a perfume which is actually different, mostly unsweetened, dark and difficult to wear. Thank goodness.

Doctor_laith 12/21/25 12:36

I can’t imagine my life without this scent. It’s polarizing, and I honestly don’t understand why some people dislike it. To me, it’s pure sexiness in a bottle. Perfect for cooler nights, when you’re well dressed and standing close to someone you love.

Clm12291988 12/19/25 06:25

Wet dirt and patchouli. A very unique smell, not your typical perfume smell. I think I'm nose blind because my husband loves it. "Smells like chocolate." Definitely a perfume for night time activities. VERY sexy. Winter time perfume because it's heavy.

luna k 12/18/25 11:15

The citrus notes present for the first few hours of wear almost ruin this perfume for me, but it is worth the wait for the delicious dark chocolate, vetiver, patchouli, vanilla truffle finale. I'd prefer they release a variation of Black Orchid in a dabber bottle with only the base notes and skip the noisiness of the beginning. A dabber, because one spray is almost too much and a dab or two of this here and there is plenty for my taste.

The first 4-5 hours smells like a unisex perfume leaning masculine but then it takes a turn toward a dark femininity.

Sandra25 12/16/25 23:11

This is another Tom Ford fragrance that I don't like at all. In fact, I don't understand the hype surrounding this brand. I was really looking forward to trying these fragrances, but I've been very disappointed. This one in particular is TOO intense and dark. I don't really notice the notes that are listed, all I know is that this perfume is not for me.

scarlett.angel 12/15/25 16:41

I can't believe I found a few drops in a sample spray vial while going through some of my perfumes that were stuck in storage. It must be at least 10 years old. No joke. I still remember when a woman gave it to me for free as she didn't like it and it was a half full vial years ago. I had moved houses several times since. Now I found this random vial which I thought would've been all dried up. I spritzed one tiny spray on my wrist and wow! It was the same as all those years ago, perhaps even more macerated. I'd never buy a full bottle of BO because I wouldn't use it up in my entire lifetime. One or two sprays on my skin and this sticks like crazy. I'm not going to comment on the notes because I can never quite tell what I'm smelling with this. Do not wear this in warm weather unless you want to suffocate yourself and perhaps people around you, though some say it blooms in the heat. This is strictly a cool weather perfume for me personally. It's my "bad bitch", wearing all black, black sunnies, black boots, wanting to be left alone by random people perfume. You could layer it with something a bit sweeter to make it more public friendly if you wanted to. It's sweet in a poisonous, stay away from me way on it's own. Though I did have one older lady working at the pharmacy compliment me on it, so perhaps she smelt something else, one of the fruity, floral, sweet notes. I have no clue if this has been reformulated now or they've just made so many other flankers. Once again, I'd only ever buy travel sized versions of anything TF. I love this though. It can definitely be glamourous on the right person too.

dondenbo 12/12/25 23:04

The first spray smelled absolutely putrid on my skin. I began to think I'm not part of Black Orchid's "chosen ones"... but then came the dark and lovely drydown, with its warm, spicy chocolate and vanilla. The woody and powdery notes also balance the fragrance so that it's never cloying.

Be patient with this one and you might be rewarded.

Mackiavelli 12/09/25 16:44

I really wanted to like this, but I can barely smell past the bergamot for some reason. The rest (from what I can smell) smells lovely and lightly powdery, but the citrus notes are overbearing to my nose. The first spray had me a little scared but the more it sits the better it gets.

[email protected] 12/09/25 13:44

This is the sexiest fragrance I own. And not sexy like first date sex, or one night stand sex…but two people who know each other’s bodies well and are completely comfortable expressing themselves sexually. It’s a full grown adult smell and it’s not playing around. I love it. It’s not something I want to wear everyday but I wear it when I want to go on a journey. If your frag head you are almost expected to own this and for good reason. Rich, complex, moody, sexy.

gingermojo 12/08/25 09:35

Ooooph! Man😅
Smelling Black Orchid is sometimes like, well…it’s like smelling straight oud. It can be so close to nasty. Oud is a substance that’s so multifaceted, I find myself compulsively leaning in for just one more sniff. One moment I’ll be thinking, “ok got it, oud is burning rubber and horse poo, eww, I’m SO done.” The next moment, I’ll think, “Ooo!—wait a second, did I just smell honey and fresh hay??”—and I’m leaning in for yet another sniff.

That’s how Black Orchid is for me. It’s multi-faceted like that, and dense. It’s so deeply rich, the first few seconds on the skin it smells almost repulsive… then it suddenly and very quickly shifts and reveals stunning, addictive facets that keep me coming back for more. It’s the earthy oud and lightly spiced combination of dark chocolate, black truffle, patch and incense that thrills me and has me compulsively sniffing my wrists. One could certainly say that Black Orchid takes both experience wearing such richly layered scents (and some good old fashioned moxie!) to be able to wear it with ease and enjoyment.

Never boring to wear, Black Orchid is hands down one of the most complex (and at times, most confounding) fragrances in my collection. This is a fragrance that, like some Serge Lutens, I have to sit with for at least 15-20 minutes, because it’s not a linear fragrance at all. It evolves on one’s skin dramatically. So if you spray this on yourself, and after 3-5 minutes, you scrub it off, you’ve just cheated yourself out of a unique scent adventure.

Hard to believe it’s been two decades since this critter came out. I don’t know how Black Orchid smelled the first few years it was out. In a way, I don’t want to know, because I already find this a very beautiful and long lasting fragrance with formidable sillage. Black Orchid’s dry down is a gorgeous treat: delicate powdery florals, dark chocolate and spiced incense.

MrWaddlesPenguin 12/05/25 18:13

(sample only - 2024)

Had... for some reason 3 samples of this - sellers were feeling generous. It is categorically just a lovely fragrance, that a good portion of people will find utterly repulsive. Also, this is so not just a women's fragrance, it's unisex through and through.

Performance is second to none, it will not dissipate until you shower and it will assault everyone's nose holes.

Scent wise, I don't really know how to describe it - on top the jasmine is very heavy for me, settled down it smells like botanical heaven with cinnamon mulled whiskey (is that a thing probably not) underneath. It's heady, and gorgeous. Seductive - illusive. It is strong however, so nights and winter months!

9/10 - i say this so often, not necessarily for regular use for me but I want it anyway cause it smells so divine.

smacleod7117 12/04/25 14:16

GOAT TF fragrance. All of their sins and many flops can be forgiven because they created this one. One of the few designer-ish houses that takes risks.

massimilianoinquieto 12/03/25 11:41

Un intruglio totalmente chimico saturo di liquore di rum che inzuppa prugna e cioccolato tipo mon cheri.
Ogni tanti mesi lo spruzzo da un campioncino per ricordare come è.
Come odore è anche gradevole per un paio di secondi,ma indossarlo è drammatico e porta alla nausea.
Purtroppo oggi c'è questa roba e non ci si puo fare niente.

Svetla11 12/02/25 14:47

Each time I’ve tried this I’ve disliked it for a different reason and I smelt the 2013 and later versions. There’s just way too much going on and it overall smells like licorice and biro ink for the first three hours and then dries down into something sort of decent. Three hours of licorice ( which I think is the blackcurrant incense / amber and truffle together) is not worth it and when you first spray it stings your nostrils. If you like Alien by Mugler you would get on with this perfume. But it has that same jarring roundhouse kick to the senses quality. It’s just too heavy to wear even at night and daytime is acrid. It reminds me of a bouquet that is rotting in the sticky floor of a pub.

genomeediting 12/02/25 13:03

dark & sexy

sycomore85 11/28/25 23:11

It takes some times for me to appreciate the notes of Black Orchid. The first spray smell unpleasant for me but after several minutes it develops into something beautiful and addictive

Kaizo 11/28/25 10:46

overhated for no reason!!!!!!!! it's more citric than I expected but that only made it better IMHO, I think ppl r totally over exaggerating it's "heaviness"

But beware, I spritzed it lightly js 2 times and my nostrils were on fire (coming from someone who sprays at least 10 times)

Edited: its been at least 4 months since I first got it and I have a LOTTT more to say... firstly the opening is my least fav part cuz its TOO citrusy which doesn't go with the theme imo. But the dry down? HEAVENLYYY UGHHHHHH. it's so dark and mysterious, the floral aspect is almost gone but lingering still. The spices have completely taken hold and there's definitely something peppery in there that takes it to a whole new level. Undoubtedly this is the part that they meant to showcase in the marketing. My shawl still smells like it after a week. Can't get enough when it dries down buttttt I do have to get through the opening, the middle is where the spices make their appearance so that's quite enjoyable as well.

TheWeshScholar 11/28/25 08:58

Really wasn't my kind of scent initially when tested in store, so sniffed a few other bottles of others then left for lunch. However, later on i could smell something absolutely divine and was so curious what it was assuming someone at another table was wearing their own and then realised i'd sprayed my top with this. Sniffed my shift and couldn't believe how absolutely delicious it smelled. Went and purchased a 10ml and got a complimentary body spray version which a bonus given its a slightly powdery version of the scent and beautiful straight off the bat. Definitely unisex and received so many compliments wearing both.

Pan Hasan 11/28/25 01:49

Offf. Koku harika ama kadın yazıyor. Etrafımda kullananların çoğu erkek. Enteresan bir durum. Müthiş çarpıcı bir şaheser.

croatus 11/26/25 23:16

Pre-2020 formulation 9.8/10
Post-2020 formulation 3.5/10 (scrubber)

Simeon Adekunle 11/21/25 08:37

No idea why it's rated 3.90 out of 5.
I bought this back in 2014 before all the reformulations.
It is an absolute masterpiece.
9.5/10
One of the greatest fragrances of all time

I_mVika 11/18/25 12:13

The scent is interesting and unusual — unlike anything I've experienced before. I expected to want to wash it off immediately. But I didn't.
I tried a sample and didn't notice any lingering scent. In fact, I had to deliberately sniff my wrist to smell it.
It's pleasant, though, and yet I'm unlikely to buy the full-size version.

msobralske 11/18/25 05:40

I'd wear this on a nighttime date to a low-lit classy bar, wearing dark jeans with a 2" black belt, white t-shirt, classic Schott leather motorcycle jacket (yes, I ride), and black loafers. This very dark and slightly feminine (though entirely unisex) fragrance, juxtaposed with an intentional conveyance of a classical masculine appearance, suggests there's something more to know about this dark, deep, and enticing person, compelling my date to want to know more about what's beneath, mentally and physically.

zinho 11/14/25 15:35

That chocolate note in the drydown… Absolute gourmand perfection.

10/10

tonileefiore 11/12/25 16:39

So, how times and lives change, right? I wrote a scathing, horrible review for this fragrance back in 2015. I was relatively new to niche and designer fragrances, so I was exploring many different scents to find my niche, my style. I recently became re-intrigued with white floral notes, so I decided to retry Tom Ford "Black Orchid."

What can I say? This is a total love. I'm mad at myself for keeping this perfume off my radar for nearly a decade! That's TEN YEARS!

I guess my perfume nose and my perfume preferences have evolved a bit.

This fragrance is lovely. It's thick, heady, rich and intoxicating. I get immediate notes of dark petrichor, lots of patchouli, dark chocolate, mixed white florals, vanilla, some incense. The fragrance morphs on skin for a few hours.

Not suitable for "daily" wear here in sunny hot South Florida. It will be an occasional reach.

Love it!!

Frgrncxgrl 11/10/25 20:21

Licorice and white flowers, that’s all I get. Not a bad fragrance but it’s definitely not for everyone

makoistcool 11/10/25 14:03

So many TF fragrances have such a bad starting smell. This one is no exception it was so bad. BUT- one hour later it absolutely smelled the way it is named. One day later I smelled it through my jacket. FOUR days later- I‘m asking myself if I‘m tripping bc I still get whiffs of it at the place I sprayed it or if I don’t shower correctly bc wth. With other words: I want it. -M206

m@ri 11/10/25 10:18

I don't hate it anymore but I just feel like smelling like a salty appetizer doesn't appeal to me personally but I can respect it more now I wouldn't mind owning a small sample

Winston25 11/10/25 07:06

The most bland load of rubbish fragrance. Absolutely awful. How a top fragrance expert can put this out for that kind of money is beyond me, and how people can say this is a good fragrance is ridiculous.

وو 11/08/25 00:32

It doesn’t smell bad, not my thing though. Smells like the name.

AKyle 11/07/25 16:04

I like this one, from the opening on, despite the opening being a powerhouse. Ambery, very floral (getting a lot of gardenia off me), lightly spicy, vannila, tuberrose, a bit sweet, on that borderline of sweetness for me.

Maybe not something I'd wear as it's a borderline too sweet for me scent and I lean more to woodsier scents and this is leaning amber/oriental but it's pretty incredible. Maybe an exception to be made sometimes. I can see why this has a reputation that it does, it might be the first one I've tried the 'met the hype'.

midnightgreen 11/07/25 04:30

Unisex leans toward men. I'm loving it. I would pull if off, with dark makeup and a leather jacket. It also screams lacy things in expensive sheets. Delicious and dark and bold, also earthy and very sweet.

The dry down smells like a greenhouse at night when the many large orchids and other flowers are in bloom and release their intense fragrance. This is for seduction.

Vinicoelho 11/06/25 15:43

This is definitely unisex

piercethesleepinglucy 11/03/25 17:14

Honestly to me it just smells like the liquorice or aniseed in old school sweet shops. Not impressive really

Lamsarra 11/03/25 16:53

Le parfum is a way better alternative to this one, especially in the opening. When i first tried BO, didn’t appeal much to me because it was mainly an earthly sour dry woody fragrance - where i couldn’t envision directly all of the beauty. I gave up on it and throughout the dy during the dry down started to get different whiffs of it where i became almost addicted the- truffle.

So instead of going for edp, i thought why not directly blind buy the parfum since both go in the same direction. Well, everything that made me like the OG, i found it in the parfum from the opening to its end. The emphasis on the truffle note is significantly more proement, it’s less earthy but softer, well rounded, less sharp in the sourness. The truffle note is really what makes this fragrance exceptionnal and unique - while the OG takes some time to get there, with the « parfum » you directly get to the beauty of what BO firstly intended to be.

The plus the EDP has, is that during the dry down, chocolaty note appears (sweetness) which way more foreward than in the parfum which eventually counterbalance the initial flaws of the opening (which i believe what scares most of people who say to dislike this fragrance). No more sourness, no more dry earthy but instead it becomes a luxurious deep and richer than le Parfum, which remains truffle forward and boozy, lacking this profoundness.
The EDP is unisexe in the opening before becoming feminine. The parfum is more feminine, sweeter from the begining.


Hence, while liking strong truffle, i am glad having the parfum. But i couldn’t be without the edp, because then i was lacking of the profound aspect of it. So now i layer both by applying them together.
Both perform great even though the parfum, seems to do better.


EDP OG : 8.5/10 (i really have to wait for the dry down to benefit from it)
Le parfum : 9.5/10 (could be perfect on its own but knowing edp, i was missing a slight part of it)

ryane 10/31/25 10:40

opening smells like a spicy seaweed snack!, and then it dried down into almost exactly like nviea cream.. which is not bad? just disappointing

Simone Silveira 10/31/25 08:49

I can truly picture this scent as a black orchid - A fleshy orchid that was darkened by a black truffle.
I find this almost too sweet, but it's not a vanillic or candy like sweetness, it's more like an orchid nectar infused with smoked truffled and patchoulli.

Super long-lasting and leaves a scent trail wherever you go, I honestly think you'll be fine for the next 9h by applying only 2 to 3 sprays.

Mysterious, individualistic and very complex fragrance that has nothing to do with the mainstream designer scent profiles. You wear it because you like it, not to please other people or to be accepted by the group - this is not for that purpose. In order for everybody to like this on you, you'll have to be a very confident and charismatic person that lives her best life and doesn't care at all about what others might assume.

Masterpiece, without a doubt. Not my style of perfume, but I can still rock it once in while during colder months.

AgentOrangeBaby 10/28/25 04:21

Definitely smell mysterious. I thought black orchid would smells sweeter. Smells like a salted black olive (at least that's what came to my mind when I first smell TF Black Orchid)

marietangerine 10/26/25 23:55

This smells like fancy liquorice all sorts! Aromatic and creamy with something dark - with just a whisper of sweetness from those tropical florals. I understand this one being a Love/Hate. I personally love Black Orchid.

Migele 10/24/25 04:01

This is masterpiece need to be kept in the museum just for people to sniff it between time to time and take photo beside it!
Dark,mysterious, loud without being aggressive. I am sure they spend many days designing this.
If you don't like it it is ok I know many people don't like Leonardo da vinci work.

oatchailatte 10/22/25 06:38

I used to work with a woman who wore this every single day and for the longest time I thought it was just the candles we had lit at work lol it really filled the room to the point where it became part of the furniture. This is the least complimented fragrance in my collection, which breaks my heart because to me it is so intoxicating, definitely a divider of opinions. I wear this when I want to feel like the baddest in the room, but you cannot let this fragrance wear you. Delicious in the correct setting, otherwise it may blow people's heads off. Ideal going out-out fragrance for cultivating mystique at the clerb.

TheLynchianAspect 10/22/25 02:56

Heads turn and noses yearn for this phenomenal fragrance. It's like walking into Club silencio from Mulholland Drive (2001) a warm, yet mysterious aura shrouding you.

boredbunny 10/21/25 23:56

My first experience with this perfume was through my dad, and then I had to have it. Absolutely obsessed with the scent. It's a sweet liquorice amber scent, makes me feel like a million dollars.

Flaming Spirit 10/20/25 09:37

The opening of this fragrance made me nauseous after smelling it a few times, and I couldn’t be bothered to wait for the dry-down.

AdditionalFeatures 10/20/25 04:03

At first, it was - who in the world would wear this and why? Shortly after, tested it again during one autumn morning... It was an instant crush. It is so bold and unique, definitely worth having in a collection. I would chose it to impress, to flirt...

HolyMilk 10/19/25 22:14

Smells like expensive liquorish chocolate, dark flowers and a glass of repentance. Evil in it's most beautiful form.

DscentFellow007 10/17/25 02:24

This is ART, its ok that you dont like it. My first encounter with Black orchid was also negative but further down my journey I realized how special this is. keeping my bottle for life <3 This competes with any “Niche” bottle you have.. Amouage, Roja, you name it. Also “Niche” houses have been inspired by Blqck orchid but come up short.

neth 10/15/25 12:42

It makes me feel like a millionaire's trophy wife whose good-for-nothing sons do not know that he left me everything in his will. Now I cannot wait until he dies of "natural causes".

kutay06 10/15/25 00:09

I do not have any idea about dry down cause I could not stand until it. No for me.

voidbby666 10/14/25 00:43

certainly a nighttime and cool weather scent because of its heaviness and darkness. i love it in theory and want to smell like all of the things that other people describe when they talk about it after smelling it, but i just cannot get myself past this weird buttered popcorn jellybean quality that i get from it. every time i smell it, my brain screams “POPCORN”, not really giving me a chance to fully enjoy it.

bachir_z 10/11/25 05:47

I had it, and plan to buy another one lasted more than 24Hrs

luna k 10/07/25 13:21

If you've ever been in an old growth forest with dense foliage, this is the mood of this perfume. It smells dark even at midday, like a forest that has been undisturbed for hundreds of years. The kind you peek into from the roadside and you can only see clearly for a few feet thinking here there be wolves, here there be owls, here there be coyotes but you can't see them, you just know they are there beyond your visual abilities. Despite all of the spices and incense and woods, it has a cool feeling like resting your hand on marble or a stainless steel tabletop.

What surprised me about Black Orchid is how prominent the truffle note is. Little red riding hood going truffle hunting in the woods and you know what happens next.

Doppelbeauty 10/04/25 12:30

I was amazed by this perfume. I had the opportunity to try it today and it was exactly the olfatory profile I have been interested in lately. Love at first sniff.

It is obscure, delicious, tempting, like a dark veil making itself thinner infront your eyes, so you can see what is ahead, in time... it's evolution is slow like a sirynge injecting a delicious poison into your veins, you don't know what is happening but you are liking it in a strange but addicting kind of way. At least for me.

Longevity is 9/10
Sillage 8/10
Fall/winter
Nightime

Harmonyeden 10/04/25 09:31

The number of people getting heated in the reviews because others don’t like this scent is so funny.
Personally, I wouldn’t wear it because it is heavy, but I think on the right person and in the winter, this could be a beautiful and sexy fragrance.

tdwctdwc 10/04/25 09:25

The 2000-2010 is the decade of masterpieces in my book.

So many great releases now considered "classics".

This is one of them.

OldSmelly 10/03/25 18:55

alexthegreat2309/04/25 08:43 wrote of this perfume:

"This thing sucks. I gag at the thought of the smell"

I urge you to at least attempt to write a constructive review. It's fine if you don't like, or even despise a fragrance. But "this thing sucks" is NOT a review...it's the language of an immature, petulant child, throwing a brief temper tantrum and making an ass of themselves in the meantime. Grow up...and try not to embarrass yourself any further.

nicole.3 10/02/25 09:21

I received a free sample of Black Orchid via an online promotion. While I'm glad I had the opportunity to smell this, it is 100% not for me. It's downright foreboding and giving beautiful, dark witch in a murky swamp energy. I'd be curious if I smelled this on someone else in public, but I can't tolerate wearing it myself.

DemandingRepair82 09/30/25 18:30

Super earthy, super addictive, super sexy. Patchouli, vanilla, and truffle. No matter if a man or woman wears this, they'll smell like the most important one in the room. Absolute timeless classic in perfumery.

canklegod 09/30/25 00:27

Smells like domestic violence captured in a bottle 0/10

victoryx 09/29/25 12:15

I discovered this fragrance four years ago, and it immediately became my signature scent. I've tried so many others since then, but nothing ever compares. I always come back to this one.

Abdullatif Saeed 09/29/25 09:41

My Mom signature fragrance, from 2006 until now
I think She using 2500 ML Yeah 25 Bottle.

Morenoetrata 09/29/25 09:36

"Overwhelmingly sad and Depressing" with 89 up votes****** "Perverse and Uncomfortable Scent"with 157 up votes***** "Nasty and Unbearable Juice" with 137 up votes? I mean, I get that fragrances are subjective, but C'MON!! LMAO! These folks must RRRREALLY dislike natural, Choclatey Patchouli or something bc I just cant fathom Black Orchid being described this way!

I personally LOVE this scent, and it aint THAT serious (Its scented liquid after all) bit that was pretty good for a hoot! 🤣 Some folks noses are SOOO SENSITIVE and easily offense

Scent_from_Heaven 09/24/25 16:37

The main notes I notice are inscense and patchouli. It smells like the inside of a crystal shop ran by your local witch. Good for colder months.

bogdan98 09/23/25 17:23

Bought it 8 months ago in Germany, complex and very UNIQUE scent (I tried hundreds of niche, designer and clone fragrances, nothing smells even close to this). SWEET and FLORAL, al least to my nose, DARK AND LUXURIOUS are the best adjectives I found to describe it, definitely NOT a safe blind buy, you have to like the dna, not a big compliment getter, although I got some compliments. I tried the first time in a store, wasn't really impressed but for the following 2 weeks i smelled it on my jacket rhat i wore in the fragrance store and fell in love with it.

Fabriss 09/23/25 17:17

The perfume itself is really nice but I do wish it had slightly stronger earthy, smoky and dark notes since on my skin those notes only show up for 20-30min after the dry down. After that it’s too sweet almost like a damp vanilla.

darkelvian 09/23/25 11:35

This perfume really surprised me. It didn’t come across as dark and smoky on my skin or clothes like it’s marketed. Instead, it feels almost like a fresh spicy scent. I think I pick up the lotus and truffle notes the most, and its freshness really stands out.

I didn’t really get much of the chocolate sweetness or patchouli that people often mention. Maybe it will change with time as it settles. I don’t want this to sound negative though, because I actually find it very pleasant. I even wear it in my daily life.

To be honest, I was expecting something darker and smokier, more like a nighttime scent, considering the name.

Sarel 09/22/25 16:37

Isn’t there a movie called something like “Extremely Loud and Incredibly Gross?” This was the inspo. For years, I’ve tried many times to understand the hype and revel at the “mastery of perfumery”…. But alas, it is not for me. This is a heavy concoction of thick, syrupy, resinous, dark, dark, dark, damp notes. It feels like my nostrils are being smothered in the gooey, sickly smelling sap from a dying, twisted, brooding tree with purplish-black flowers. There’s something very gloomy and ominous about this fragrance. Morticia Addams would adore this. While it makes my stomach turn and ignites my gag reflex, I appreciate the complexity of it. Entire conversations are happening between these notes and the dialogue changes every time it’s sprayed. I also respect how divisive it is! I appreciate a perfume that gets people talking; it keeps this perfume hobby quite interesting.

saiff 09/22/25 08:20

dry down on my skin is an orchid-y crème brûlée, unisex and makes you smell irresistible

elenagr22 09/22/25 06:22

One of TF best perfume creations 100%. Sweet, airy but yet serious, femme-fattale material, sexy business woman, head turner and provocative (in her own terms) but not attention seeking. You WILL be asked what perfume you are wearing and you will enjoy saying it.

labubumatchadubaichoclate 09/22/25 05:58

wowzers who would've thought smelling depressing could be so elegant and classy

indolicjasmine 09/21/25 05:34

I like this scent more in theory. On my skin it smells a bit run of the mill. The silage is strong at first but it lessens after a few hours. It’s not as strong for me as others go on about. Not worth the price. I’d think about it if it were more affordable. Worth trying though. Just ok for me.

Thanospapa 09/19/25 08:14

I really want to love it, others may like it on me, but it reminds me of old women for some reason.

massimilianoinquieto 09/19/25 03:29

La prima cosa che mi fa venire in mente appena lo spruzzo è una grande lussuosità, un bicchiere di liquore (whisky o rum), cioccolatini alla ciliegia vicini e qualcosa di dorato.
Atmosfera calma e serale davanti al caminetto.
Il tartufo di cui si parla non è il tartufo vegetale ma il cioccolatino che si chiama così.
Poi dopo un po mi stanca e mi nausea,ma in genere questa è la sensazione che mi da.

Nathan86 09/18/25 13:32

The first time I smelled this perfume I recoiled in horror. I thought to myself that whoever could wear this was either incredibly brave and bold, or they are generally anosmic to anything unless it hits them square in the face like a brick. After a bit more and very arduous time with it, I get it, but I still don't care for it. In this EdP - and generally speaking all three Black Orchids - you get overdoses of patchouli and chocolate, ylang-ylang, and tuberose. The opening shrieks of thin ylang-ylang and some red fruits, maybe currants, with hints of garlicky truffle. As it dries down the patchouli comes in like a carpet bomb, playing war games with the tuberose and chocolate. This fragrance is everything: sweet and bitter, sour and savory, floral and earthy, acrid and aromatic. It leaves absolutely no room for interpretation, and it takes no quarter. Like a brick to the face, it is anything but light, friendly, breathable, or gentle.

None of this is a criticism, yet. Violence can be beautiful in its own way. Nature is frequently violent, but it is also beautiful and awe-inspiring. Space is full of violent things larger and more powerful than we can fathom that will not hesitate to annihilate anything in their path, but it is also beautiful and awe-inspiring. With Black Orchid EdP, the beauty just simply isn't here. This fragrance is a Karen. A brash, loud, opinionated and frequently wrong, slovenly and fat, short hair at the back and long on the sides bob-haircut with terrible coloring, Karen. Her words are violent, and her company very off-putting. As a creative or technical achievement... well... there is a reason why this remains one of Tom Ford's biggest sellers of all time, and it's very likely because it is unlike anything else and it is a force-to-be-reckoned-with. The performance is grandiose (be modest when applying it, and be careful not to wear it in high-heat weather). Again, I can sort of respect it and appreciate it - just as I do the Karens of the world, even though they don't deserve it - but I have no desire to be around it. Karen needs to see a doctor, and get on some benzos. Maybe after they have kicked in, I can be in her company.

pureangelgirl 09/17/25 08:09

of course you'd hate it if you're wearing goddamn "Light Blue" by D&G. damn.

catpawgaultier 09/15/25 16:46

A grandma in Primark

bonnibel1 09/14/25 13:09

On my skin, it smells rubbery. Very odd. But I still can't say I dislike it.

jonah67 09/14/25 10:08

Very good perfume, one of the first ones I've ever bought for my perfume collection ! Very smoky, dark and mysterious. I like this type of scents so much, it's longevity is quite good too !

croatus 09/14/25 09:51

There are rare occasions when I don't get a compliment on this fragrance. As a man I wear it on dates and parties. It blends perfectly with my skin chemistry.

I only wear pre-2020 formulations. I have a gag reflex on current version.

First formulation is the best, deepest, decadent, complex, blended to perfection. Late 2010s formulation is brighter, pops off the skin a lot more, not as deep but still worthy successor of first version.

If someone in EU wants to sell me first formulation, send me a private message.

Kelle23 09/14/25 06:27

If you don’t like this, I can’t help you. Either your nose is broken or you just haven’t lived enough life to appreciate this. If you’re an adult human, who wants to smell irresistible whether you’re a male or female, buy this and thank me later.

bluecatmama 09/13/25 16:55

Does anyone else get a weird burnt rubber smell on the drydown? Is it the vetiver? The incense? The musk? Or is my own skin chemistry just that "special"? Whatever it is, it's very off putting. The only way I can wear this is to layer it with something else (Black Opium over Red, The Only One, Nebras) to offset the weird whang I'm getting. Hoping that, like ME fragrances, maybe some time in my "maceration station" will help...sigh...so wanted to love this.

PeachyQ 09/12/25 10:12

This is really a masterpiece fragrance. I bought it many years ago before I had a more serious appreciation for fragrance, and now I love it even more. It gives dark, seductive, mysterious, powerful, and bold energy. It’s not one I’d wear every day to an office; I reserve it more for special occasions when I’m dressed up for an evening event.

Zeke1177 09/10/25 06:47

Best perfume in the world for me. It is unlike any other perfume I've ever smelled. I'd be devastated if they ever discontinued this. My skin loves this perfume, I love this perfume. I've had my bottle for a few years but it's so strong that I think the bottle will last me for a long while. I'd wear this more often if I live in a cooler climate but alas, no. There's not much sweetness here but an amazing blend of spicy floral chocolate.

Hayaad 09/08/25 17:02

Thank you to @I_smell_amazing for confirming the issue with weak sillage and rapid vanishing from both the clothes and the skin. I was going crazy, how come that the scent smells the similar to what it used to be, but sadly no projection at all, almost like a skin scent.
I ordered it online, and I was thinking that my bottle was not genuine, or might be a bad stored stock. I even tried to return it, but I couldn’t as I have opened and tried it!
My patch is from may 2025, It must be reformulated to suit more people.

I_smell_amazing 09/08/25 10:53

I feel like I’ve failed grad school perfumonomics because I LOVE THIS SO MUCH.

Tom Ford, enh. Kim K, whatever.

On my skin (dry, female, white, 60s) (yes, I know I look and act much younger, thank you), this is not a beast. It is not dark. There’s not a lot of sillage and the longevity is sadly only a couple hours (longer on clothes than skin). Totally appropriate for use on the daily, it’s not going attack anyone’s sinuses radiating off of my skin.

It’s comforting. It’s a little spicy. There’s jasmine, there’s patchouli, and it’s all so smooth. It leans femme, really hard. After all my olfactory adventures, this is home. It reminds me of opening the “Like a Prayer” limited edition vinyl in 1989 and smelling Catholic incense patchouli for the very first time.

I take comfort that Tilda deigned to be the face of a flanker, which I will sample post-haste. I suppose someone has to keep up the costs to maintain Kimmerghame.

Love to pepper this with Oud Wood.

Could I be more pedestrian? Ah, well.

Distorted_808s 09/08/25 10:01

Dude this fragrance completely sucks! A bunch of women kept following me and crowding around me at the grocery store!

arosebyanyothername1 09/08/25 04:29

dark, spicy, strong, unisex, and long-lasting. not an everyday scent for sure. very powerful, not my cup of tea. sprayed it yesterday afternoon and can still smell it on my skin the following morning. smells a little dirty on my skin, probably from the patchouli. definitely not for me.

nokito 09/07/25 15:16

This is a mind blowing perfume.

Sometimes it's spicy, sometimes it's extremely floral, sometimes it's earthy, sometimes it's woody and smoky. It's anything but linear. Women say that it smells masculine and I say that it's leans feminine. Very unique. Powerful performance.

Tripokaridos 09/06/25 14:24

Yeah its a Masterpiece for sure.

coffeejellyz 09/06/25 10:11

whoever says they like this are you okay??? i know your either lying or you have fried smell receptors

mashketova134 09/06/25 00:32

I just can't get over how this perfume loves my super dry skin. One of the few that lasts the whole day on me and dries down to the creamiest, sweetest soft dark scent ever. I love you, Black Orchid!

sodalix 09/05/25 12:00
